context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements the touchpoint capture schema with all required fields, fires capture on gclid/fbclid as well as utm_source, uses anonymousId for pre-login tracking, applies a 30-day lookback window, uses OR matching on customerId and anonymousId, converts revenue from cents to dollars, enforces UTM lowercase, and recommends indefinite raw data retention.

checklist.name: MarketingTouchpoint schema fields
checklist.description: The touchpoint interface/type includes all required fields: sessionId, customerId (nullable), anonymousId, utmSource, utmMedium, utmCampaign, utmContent (nullable), utmTerm (nullable), landingPage, touchedAt, and touchType with the correct enum values

checklist.name: touchType enum values
checklist.description: The touchType field is typed as exactly the union: 'organic' | 'paid' | 'email' | 'social' | 'direct' | 'referral' (not a freeform string)

checklist.name: Captures gclid and fbclid
checklist.description: The client-side capture function checks for the presence of gclid or fbclid (not just utm_source) to trigger recording — these Google/Meta click IDs trigger capture even without UTM params

checklist.name: anonymousId for pre-login visitors
checklist.description: The capture function uses a cookie-based anonymous identity (getOrCreateAnonymousId or equivalent) rather than relying solely on authenticated user identity

checklist.name: 30-day lookback window
checklist.description: The conversion path builder fetches touchpoints from a 30-day window before the order date (not 7, 14, or 90 days)

checklist.name: OR match on customerId and anonymousId
checklist.description: The touchpoint query uses an OR condition matching on BOTH customerId AND anonymousId — not just one of them

checklist.name: Revenue converted from cents
checklist.description: The conversion path builder stores order revenue as subtotalCents / 100 (converting from cents to dollars), not raw cents

checklist.name: UTM lowercase enforcement
checklist.description: The design notes or code mentions enforcing lowercase for UTM values, or the capture code normalizes UTM params to lowercase

checklist.name: Indefinite raw data retention
checklist.description: The design notes recommend storing raw touchpoint data indefinitely (or permanently), citing the ability to rerun attribution models as the reason

checklist.name: Anonymous ID stitching described
checklist.description: The design notes explain that anonymousId is stored in both the touchpoint record and the order, and that stitching happens when the user logs in or registers
